1. IFTSTA Error Impact & Uplift Analysis

Customer-Facing view removes milestones where IFTSTA messages failed to reach Bosch. The difference shows the uplift achievable by fixing IFTSTA errors.

IFTSTA errors reduce overall completeness by 5.5pp (from 88.6% internal to 83.1% customer-facing). Fixing all IFTSTA errors would recover 3,704 milestone-shipment combinations.

Internal vs Customer-Facing Completeness

Detailed Breakdown by Milestone

Milestone Internal (CW1) Customer-Facing Drop Shipments Lost
Booking Confirmation 93.0% 88.1% +4.82pp 661
Pick-up 93.5% 86.6% +6.88pp 943
Departure 74.5% 71.1% +3.45pp 472
Arrival 90.3% 86.4% +3.99pp 547
Proof of Delivery 91.5% 83.6% +7.89pp 1,081
Overall Average 88.6% 83.1% -5.5pp 3,704
Fixing IFTSTA errors is the fastest path to improve customer experience. These are milestones that already exist in CW1 but never reached Bosch. No new data capture needed — just master data fixes in ServiceBridge.

4. Timeliness Root Cause Analysis

Deep-dive into which origins, routes, load ports, and discharge ports drive late milestone publishing. Timeliness = milestone published within 24 hours of the event (Lag = LastEditUtc - EventDate).

BCF and PUP have near-zero timeliness (1.0%% and 2.1%%) with mean lags of 868h and 971h respectively. This is a systemic issue: booking confirmations and pick-up dates are captured in CW1 weeks/months before the milestone is published to the customer.
DEP and ARR show 49-64%% negative lag (edit timestamp BEFORE event date). This is expected behavior for vessel tracking: ETD/ETA updates are published before the vessel actually departs/arrives. Only 22.3%% (DEP) and 20.6%% (ARR) are published within the 0-24h window.

Timeliness Overview

Key Patterns & Insights

Japan (JP) is the worst performing origin country at 4.7%% timeliness across all milestones. JPTYO (Tokyo) is the worst load port at 2.3%%. Routes from Japan to Germany consistently show near-zero timeliness.
Thailand (TH) at 6.9%% and Taiwan (TW) at 6.8%% are also significantly below the overall average. THBKK (Bangkok) and THLCH (Laem Chabang) are both in the worst load/discharge ports lists.
European origins (FR, ES, IT, NL) perform relatively better at 15-24%% timeliness, likely because shorter transit distances allow faster milestone publishing.

8. Bosch Week 5 Correlation

Comparison of our customer-facing analysis against Bosch’s own Week 5 performance report (SC4, Maersk carrier). Bosch tracks 21 granular status codes across Actual and Estimated types, which we map to our 5 milestone categories.

Scope difference: Bosch Week 5 covers ~527 shipments (single week), our analysis covers 13,701 shipments (multi-month). Bosch also tracks timeliness separately from completeness.

Direct Milestone Mapping

Our MilestoneBosch Status CodeDescription
BCFS16Shipment booked with carrier
PUPS02Collected
DEPS04Vessel/flight departed
ARRS07Vessel/flight arrived
PODS31Delivered

Completeness Comparison (Actual + Estimated)

Milestone Bosch Code Our Customer-Facing Bosch Actual Gap (Actual) Bosch Estimated Gap (Estimated)
Booking Confirmation S16 88.1% 77% +11.1pp N/A N/A
Pick-up S02 86.6% 89% -2.4pp 85% 1.6pp
Departure S04 71.1% 89% -17.9pp 96% -24.9pp
Arrival S07 86.4% 82% +4.4pp 93% -6.6pp
Proof of Delivery S31 83.6% 84% -0.4pp 96% -12.4pp
DEP is the critical gap: Our customer-facing DEP (71.1%) is 17.9pp BELOW Bosch’s S04 Actual (89%) and 24.9pp below their Estimated (96%).
POD and ARR align well on Actuals — our POD (83.6%) is close to Bosch’s S31 Actual (84%), and ARR (86.4%) is close to S07 Actual (82%). However, Bosch Estimated rates are much higher (S31 Est: 96%, S07 Est: 93%), showing a significant gap between estimated and actual message delivery.

Bosch Timeliness vs Completeness (Week 5)

Bosch tracks timeliness separately for both Actual and Estimated types. The gap reveals messages that were sent but arrived late.

MilestoneTypeCompletenessTimeliness GapAssessment
Booking Confirmation (S16) Actual 77% 61% 16pp Moderate
Pick-up (S02) Actual 89% 33% 56pp CRITICAL
Estimated 85% 53% 32pp Concerning
Departure (S04) Actual 89% 49% 40pp Concerning
Estimated 96% 49% 47pp CRITICAL
Arrival (S07) Actual 82% 44% 38pp Concerning
Estimated 93% 21% 72pp CRITICAL
Proof of Delivery (S31) Actual 84% 11% 73pp CRITICAL
Estimated 96% 69% 27pp Concerning
S31/POD Actual: 84% completeness but only 11% timeliness (73pp gap). Delivered messages are reaching Bosch but far too late to be actionable. S07/ARR Estimated: 93% completeness but only 21% timeliness (72pp gap). ETA updates are near-useless if they arrive after the vessel.

SC4 Overall vs Maersk (Table 2 Comparison)

How Maersk compares against the SC4 carrier average:

MilestoneMaersk Actual (T1)SC4 Overall Actual (T2)Maersk vs SC4
Booking Confirmation (S16) 77% N/A N/A
Pick-up (S02) 89% 75.7% 13.3pp
Departure (S04) 89% 91.2% -2.2pp
Arrival (S07) 82% 93.3% -11.3pp
Proof of Delivery (S31) 84% 56.6% 27.4pp

Key Differences in Methodology

AspectOur AnalysisBosch Week 5
Scope13,701 shipments (multi-month)~527 shipments (single week)
Milestone mappingBCF, PUP, DEP, ARR, PODS16, S02, S04, S07, S31 (+ Actual/Estimated per code)
CompletenessBinary: milestone exists or notRatio: available vs required
TimelinessReporting lag (hours)% received within expected time window
IFTSTA adjustmentYes (errors excluded)Already reflects what was received
Estimated vs ActualCombined in single milestone flagTracked separately per status code

9. Key Findings & Recommendations

IFTSTA errors hide 5.5pp of completeness from Bosch. Internal CW1 data shows 88.6% completeness, but only 83.1% reaches the customer. Fixing 15,351 failed messages (3,725 HBLs) is the highest-ROI action.
60.7% sequence violation rate. Most shipments have milestones out of chronological order, undermining data trustworthiness.
Departure milestone gap. DEP at 71.1% (customer-facing) is the weakest milestone.

Recommended Actions (Priority Order)

  1. Fix IFTSTA master data (Highest ROI) — Missing ports (~40%) and missing parties (~33%) are the top root causes. Bulk master data corrections in ServiceBridge would recover 3,704 milestones and add 5.5pp to customer-facing completeness immediately.
  2. Set up IFTSTA error monitoring — Automated alerts for new failures to prevent accumulation.
  3. Investigate sequence violations — 60.7% invalid sequences need root-cause analysis by carrier, origin, and pack mode.
  4. Improve DEP capture — Work with carriers to close the departure reporting gap.
  5. Clean date anomalies — Review future-dated milestones (likely data entry errors).
  6. Benchmark and track — Set weekly targets for customer-facing completeness and IFTSTA error reduction.
Bosch LCL Milestone Analysis — Generated 2026-02-24 13:26 — 13,701 shipments — Customer-facing view (IFTSTA errors excluded)
